CommunityApplications.Profile:
Translarr is a self-hosted subtitle translator that plugs into Sonarr, Radarr, Emby, and Jellyfin.
When an import lands with subtitles in the wrong language (Russian fansubs on a Japanese anime, hardcoded Spanish on a Korean drama, etc.), Translarr extracts the track, translates it with a context-aware LLM, adapts the timing for the target language's reading rate, and drops a clean `.srt` next to the video.
**Features:** - LLM-powered translation (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, DeepSeek, Gemini, or local Ollama — free) - Automatic reading-rate adaptation so subtitles stay readable - Tag-based opt-in via Sonarr/Radarr — only translates what you tag - On-demand translation from Emby/Jellyfin player subtitle modal - 6 translation presets: Quick & Cheap, Balanced, Best Quality, Local & Free, DeepSeek Budget, Gemini Flash - Cost guards: daily and per-job spend caps - Push notifications via ntfy.sh - Web UI: dashboard, job history, settings, library browser, glossary editor - All settings are live-mutable — no container restart needed
